class.name: MultiNodeSub
class.brief_description: Base class for all MultiNode child nodes. Handles parent discovery, active filtering, per-instance data, and compute shaders.
class.description:
MultiNodeSub is the base for every child that attaches to a MultiNode parent. It manages which instances this node processes via two filtering layers (instance_every and instance_range) ANDed together, owns a per-instance float data array, and can optionally run a GLSL compute shader each frame. Must be a direct child of a MultiNode to function.
Active filtering uses two layers combined with AND logic: - [b]instance_every[/b]: Coarse pattern filter (every Nth instance). - [b]instance_range[/b]: Excel-style range string for fine-grained inclusion/exclusion.
An instance is active only if it passes both filters. For runtime overrides beyond these two layers, use [method set_instance_active] to directly manipulate the active array.

members.member: Read-only. The number of instances currently active on this node after applying all filters.

Controls which instances this node processes. 1 = all instances. 2 = every other instance (0, 2, 4...). 3 = every third (0, 3, 6...). Negative values select the complement: -2 processes the instances that +2 skips (1, 3, 5...). 0 = disabled (no instances processed). Use paired positive/negative values on two nodes to split work across LOD levels.

Excel-style range string for fine-grained instance filtering. Tokens are comma-separated and processed left-to-right. Examples: - [code]0:99[/code] — include indices 0 through 99 - [code]1:10,15,20:30[/code] — include 1-10, 15, and 20-30 - [code]-5[/code] — everything except index 5 - [code]0:99,-50:59[/code] — indices 0-99 except 50-59 - [code]1:10,-3,17:20,-18[/code] — 1-10 (minus 3), plus 17-20 (minus 18) Empty string means no restriction (all instances pass).

GLSL compute shader source code. Runs once per instance per frame on a local RenderingDevice. The shader receives: binding 0 = transforms (3 x vec4 per instance, read/write), binding 1 = active flags (uint per instance, read), binding 2 = instance data (sub_data_stride floats per instance, read/write). Push constants: delta (float), time (float), count (uint), stride (uint).
Enable or disable the compute shader dispatch. The shader compiles when this is enabled and compute_code is set. Disable to stop GPU dispatch without losing the shader code.

method.name: set_instance_active
method.description: Directly set the active state of a single instance. Writes to the computed active array, bypassing the instance_every and instance_range filters. Note: the next call to set_instance_every or set_instance_range will recompute and overwrite this override.

method.name: is_instance_active
method.description: Check whether a specific instance is active on this node (after all filters).

method.name: set_all_active
method.description: Set all instances to active or inactive at once. Directly writes to the active array, bypassing filters.

method.name: get_active_count
method.description: Returns the number of currently active instances on this node.

method.name: get_multi_node_parent
method.description: Returns the parent MultiNode, or null if this node is not a child of one.

method.name: get_data_stride
method.description: Returns this node's per-instance data stride (floats per instance). Inherited from the parent MultiNode's sub_data_stride setting.

method.name: set_instance_data
method.description: Set a single float in this node's per-instance data array. Channel is the offset (0 to data_stride-1). This data is private to this sub-node — not shared with siblings.

method.name: get_instance_data
method.description: Read a single float from this node's per-instance data array.

method.name: set_instance_data_vec
method.description: Set the entire data vector for a single instance on this node.

method.name: get_instance_data_vec
method.description: Get the full data vector for a single instance on this node.
